objectives: Identify potential entry points and services
tools: Directory traversal tools (e.g., Dirb, Burp Suite)
success_criteria: All target domains have been discovered and mapped
Key services have been identified and documented

objectives: Identify potential vulnerability classes for each identified service
Research existing threat intelligence and public reports
tools: Threat intelligence platforms (e.g., IBM X-Force, Google Threat Analysis Center)
Public bug bounty reports and research papers
success_criteria: Potential vulnerability classes have been identified for each service
Existing threat intelligence and public reports have been researched and documented

objectives: Test identified vulnerability classes with payloads
tools: Development and testing of custom payloads
success_criteria: Vulnerabilities have been confirmed and payloads have been tested
Exploits have been successfully demonstrated on local-forks

objectives: Determine the potential impact and severity of each vulnerability
tools: Vulnerability scanner and reporter plugins (e.g., Nessus, OpenVAS)
success_criteria: Findings have been validated and prioritized
Each vulnerability has been assigned a severity score

objectives: Document and prepare submissions for bounty claims
Ensure compliance with program rules and guidelines
tools: Bug bounty reporting templates and frameworks (e.g., Immunefi, HackerOne)
Documentation and reporting tools (e.g., Google Docs, Notion)
success_criteria: Submissions have been thoroughly documented and prepared
All program rules and guidelines have been followed

priority_targets: Smart contract vulnerabilities in tBTC smart contracts
Governance vulnerabilities in governance smart contracts
Cross-chain bridge security vulnerabilities in tBTC cross-chain bridge
risk_considerations: Potential impact and severity of identified vulnerabilities
